steps.title: Identify the decisive signal
steps.content: The phrase “guilds were much more numerous relative to the population in some cities than in others” requires an example that contrasts two cities with different guild-to-population ratios.

steps.title: Match the data to the claim
steps.content:
Choice C directly compares Rouen (1 guild per 661 inhabitants) to Paris (1 guild per 4,511 inhabitants), showing a roughly 7-fold difference in how densely guilds were distributed—exactly the type of variation the sentence claims to illustrate.

Choices A, B, and
  • D either distort the data or lack the necessary comparison: A falsely states the cities had nearly equal inhabitants
  • B incorrectly claims Lyon had more guilds than Paris
  • D provides only Paris's figures without showing a contrast
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
